data.content: Implementation review rejected. Three violations of user requirements: (1) INCOMPLETE THEME COVERAGE: The conversation-log-theme.ts helper only covers ConversationLogHeader, SessionPicker, and ConsolePanelContent shell. SessionCard (SessionCardShell, SessionCardHeader, PromptEntryRow, SessionBoundaryRow, EmptySessionMessage), ResponseEntryRow (ResponseEntryHeader, RawEventDetails), InteractionPicker (InteractionDropdown, InteractionOption), and FilterDropdown (FilterDropdownMenu, FilterOptionRow, FilterDropdownFooter) all still have hardcoded dark palette classes (#1a1a2e, #16162a, #e0e0e0, text-white/xx, border-white/10). These render as dark boxes inside a light container. (2) FONT READABILITY: Because subcomponents are not themed, text in session cards, prompts, responses, interaction picker, and filter dropdown is light-on-dark (e.g. text-[#e0e0e0] on bg-[#16162a]) regardless of theme toggle. The user reports tab fonts, subtitle bar text, and model/timer text are near-impossible to read. (3) DEBUGGER LAYOUT: The History Debugger remains in a grid-cols-2 50/50 split with the console panel, squashing the embedded terminal. User requests converting to a full-width tab-based layout where Debug gets the entire ConversationLog width. Plan at .claude/plans/toasty-wibbling-blum.md details the recommended implementation approach for all three fixes.
